title: Penguoers (Joke)
wikitext:
{{DISPLAYTITLE:Entity P: "Penguoers"}} {{Joke}}{{Warningbox|content=Yay, new Partygoer rewrite!!!!}}{{Underline}} {{Blurimage|file=Penguoer (Joke).jpg|hovertext=Chinstrap|caption=An image of a chinstrap Penguoer.}} ==Description== '''Entity P''', nicknamed as '''Penguoers''', refers to the majority of individuals infected by '''Pinguine Avibus Virus''' ('''PAV'''). Their appearance is largely consistent, resembling waddling, feathered fowls completely covered with a large monochrome three-piece suit and top hat. Their large beaks often poke through the black-and-white garments, giving a plague-doctorly appearance to their face. Why infected victims display these behaviours so consistently remains unknown, though it is theorised that the '''PAV''' pathogen may possess an animalous, mind-altering property that drives the behaviour of victims to such a degree of similarity. Interestingly, those infected outside the '''Penguin Rooms''' do not follow these behaviours, instead waddling around with their feathers unobscured. Individuals with late-stage '''PAV''' fixate on locating and feeding on seafood, primarily fish, squid and crustaceans, both as a means of spreading the virus further and coping with their insatiable hunger. This behaviour often leads to violent attacks, causing rapid transmission to survivors. Their aggressive demeanour does not remain consistent when encountering other carriers of the virus, unfortunately allowing the formation of groups known as "'''huddles'''". Carriers of '''PAV''' exhibit clear symptoms that can be easily spotted if one is aware of what to look for; common indicators include obnoxious, unintelligible squawking and a distinctive waddling gait. Due to their unpredictable behaviour, getting within flipper's reach of an infected host is exceedingly dangerous and should be avoided at all costs. {{Blurimage|file=Pinguine Avibus Virus (Joke).jpeg|hovertext=Embryo|caption=A microscopic view of the Pinguine Avibus pathogen.|align=left}} ==Virology== '''Pinguine Avibus Virus''' ('''PAV''') is an animalous infectious disease caused by a viral pathogen lingering throughout '''Level Fowl'''. It has a relatively quick incubation period, with victims often experiencing initial symptoms within a matter of hours after infection, although its initial stages are easy to misdiagnose. At a microscopic level, the pathogen seems to appear as a penguin embryo. It is not known how or why the virus maintains this form, as it is far removed from the structure of any other known microscopic organism. While most of '''Level Fowl''' carries little presumed risk of contracting '''PAV''', significant caution must be taken when entering the '''Penguin Rooms''' due to the increased presence of the pathogen. Reports of feather-sprouting, beak growth, and other early signs of infection are occasionally reported by those exploring it, with many cases quickly worsening over time. Further investigation has revealed that, rather than the strains being a foreign body introduced by wanderers, the level appears to propagate the virus itself; how exactly this process occurs is currently unknown, but it presents a significant hazard due to the infection's contagious nature. Efforts to locate and quarantine those who escape the '''Penguin Rooms''' have achieved minimal success due to its lack of a consistent exit point, but further options are soon to be explored. Infected individuals often do not manage to exit the level past '''Auk Two''' of the virus, instead being left to wander the level's halls as a mere husk, searching for seafood unlucky enough to stumble upon them. ==Auks== '''PAV''' is comprised of four progressive stages, also known as '''auks''', following initial contamination, becoming more severe with time. As the virus progresses, cognitive abilities significantly deteriorate, leaving wanderers with little to no control over their actions as animalistic shells of their former selves. ===Auk One (The Faerie Rookery)=== '''Auk One''' often goes unnoticed by infected individuals, simply involving mild feelings of soreness, tiredness, and aching due to the feathers trying to burst their way through the skin. The sensation has been described as no worse than a stomachache, with light cramping and irritation taking place in the first few hours after infection. As such, the infection often goes entirely unnoticed at this auk, with the majority of individuals progressing into the second auk before their symptoms become apparent. ===Auk Two (The Rockhopper Rookery)=== '''Auk Two''' involves gradually worsening symptoms, with infected individuals often reporting longing for water, growing feelings of skin irritation and hunger for seafood, and generally feather-brained mental functions. Awareness of infection in this stage is crucial, as early and effective action can prevent or halt its continued development. In many cases, wanderers have managed to recover from '''Auk Two''' of '''PAV''' by quarantining themselves from further exposure to the strain. Victims often experience an intensified feeling of hunger, causing them to rapidly consume any sources of seafood they have access to. Other symptoms in this stage involve increased feelings of anxiety, aching in various parts of the body (primarily the front of the face due to beak growth), and a general desire to be near water. As '''Auk Two''' progresses, the sensation of seafood hunger only worsens, with no amount of nourishment managing to satisfy it. Infected victims often resort to consuming cans of tuna once all living sources have been depleted, regardless of their lack of nutrients. The agonising hunger often pushes victims to perform voluntary crustacean consumption, marking their progression into '''Auk Three''' and serving as the point of no return. ===Auk Three (The Chinstrap Rookery)=== The chances of recovery from '''Auk Three''' and beyond are slim to none. In this stage, the growing sensation of hunger for seafood becomes unbearable and takes complete hold of an individual's mind. One's self-control and sense of humanity are compromised in the search for fish, squid and crustaceans; in desperation, victims will begin to transform fully, progressing from their feathers to their beaks and flippers. Agony and fear are prevalent as they begin to lose their hold on reality, drifting in and out of lucidity into hallucinations of the Antarctic landscape in [[the Frontrooms]]. Their suffering and mental warfare remain until the infection eventually takes full control of their limbic system. The victim is reduced to a vessel for the virus as they progress into '''Auk Four''', their actions driven solely by the animalistic brain of the penguin. {{Blurimage|file=Emperor Penguoer (Joke).jpeg|hovertext=Degradation|caption=A Penguoer in the final auk of PAV.}} ===Auk Four (The Emperor Rookery)=== '''Auk Four''' marks the victim's transition from sapience to complete penguin. An individual in this stage is thought to have no remaining higher-level cognition. Little humanity remains, not even their appearance, which is often likened to a feathered husk once the infection has taken total control over its host. Victims are known to begin obnoxiously squawking at this stage, making incomprehensible cawing noises under their breath as they wander. Due to their impaired vision when on land, infected individuals typically depend on auditory and olfactory cues as a means of navigation whilst not submerged in water. This stage also serves as the distinction between '''Penguoers''' and basic carriers; this is primarily determined by their location, as those within the '''Penguin Rooms''' strangely take to covering themselves with a three-piece suit and top hat, and waiting until they encounter another living source of seafood, more akin to a bird than a mammal. {| class="fandom-table" |+
Distinguishing Features
|- ! '''Carriers''' ! '''Penguoers''' |- | Present across several levels | Present only in the '''Penguin Rooms''' |- | Still considered human | Considered a penguin by most |- | Feathers are visible | Feathers are obscured with a three-piece suit and top hat |} ==Guidelines== ===Do:=== *Remember the warning signs of '''PAV''' and where it may be waddling. *Keep a reasonable distance away from anyone who may be infected with '''PAV'''. ===Do Not:=== *Risk contamination or infection in any way. *Attempt to make contact with an infected individual in any way; if you can hear them squawking, you are too close. *Make noise near a full '''Penguoer''' or individual in the late stages of '''PAV'''; they primarily rely on sound to identify others. *Smell bad near a full '''Penguoer''' or individual in the late stages of '''PAV'''; they primarily rely on smell to identify others. ---- {{Collapse|num=1|initial=
